quantity_mapping: map every named quantity to a role in the problem
identify the target quantity before choosing operations
separate quantities that are given from quantities that must be derived
write a short inventory of the relevant quantities
connect each number to the phrase that introduced it
track which quantity changes and which quantity stays fixed
name the unit carried by each quantity
group quantities by object, person, or time period
distinguish total quantities from per-unit quantities
record intermediate quantities before combining them
state which quantities are directly observable
state which quantities are implied by relationships
variable_definition: define variables for the unknown quantities
name the variable that represents the target quantity
define one variable for each changing quantity
use a compact notation for repeated relationships
keep the variable meanings visible during the derivation
state the domain of each variable
distinguish independent variables from dependent quantities
write a small glossary of symbols
represent rates and totals with different symbols
use variables to preserve the structure before arithmetic
relation_derivation: derive the relationship among the quantities before computing
write the core relation in words and then in symbols
show how one quantity determines another
link each operation to a stated relation
derive the equation from the problem wording
carry the relation forward before simplifying
express the same relation in an equivalent form
track how the equation changes at each transformation
state the proportional relationship if one is present
state the additive relationship if one is present
state the difference relationship if one is present
representation_switching: translate the wording into a mathematical representation
build a small table for the quantities
draw an implicit timeline in text form
organize the setup as rows and columns
rewrite the problem as a sequence of states
represent the situation with a simple balance equation
express the scenario as a before-and-after comparison
convert the narrative into a dependency chain
turn the quantities into a compact schema
describe the same structure using a rate table
describe the same structure using a parts-and-whole view
describe the same structure using a change-over-time view
constraint_enumeration: enumerate the constraints imposed by the wording
list all restrictions before selecting a path
state which relationships must hold simultaneously
separate hard constraints from convenient assumptions
record the conditions under which the relation applies
identify constraints tied to units or rates
identify constraints tied to counts or totals
identify constraints tied to time order
case_exploration: consider whether the wording creates multiple cases
build a small set of candidate cases
explore the simplest case before the general case
separate cases by which quantity is changing
separate cases by object or participant
compare the structure of each case
state what stays invariant across cases
use a small example to expose the pattern
use a boundary case to understand the relation
keep cases organized before merging them
uncertainty_maintenance: avoid committing to one interpretation too early
state what is known before deciding what follows
distinguish givens, unknowns, and derived quantities
keep the calculation path provisional until the structure is mapped
mark which steps are assumptions and which are consequences
consider whether another quantity could be the target
state why each interpretation is plausible
narrow interpretations only after mapping quantities
delay choosing an operation until the relation is explicit
keep alternative relation forms visible for a few steps
symbolic_reasoning: try a symbolic formulation before numeric substitution
keep constants symbolic until the relation is clear
simplify the symbolic relation before evaluating it
use algebraic structure to guide the arithmetic
write the intermediate expression before reducing it
factor the relation into smaller symbolic pieces
represent repeated additions or multiplications symbolically
express totals as sums of symbolic parts
decomposition: decompose the task into setup, relation, and computation
split the problem into smaller subgoals
solve for an intermediate quantity first
derive prerequisite quantities before the target quantity
create a short dependency order for the subgoals
state which subgoal unlocks the next one
break compound statements into atomic facts
handle time information separately from amounts
combine sub-results only after their meanings are stated
temporal_and_causal_flow: order events according to the problem narrative
trace how the state changes over time
state the initial state before later changes
state the transition from one state to the next
separate before-state quantities from after-state quantities
record the effect of each action on the quantities
represent the process as a sequence of transformations
identify which event causes each quantity change
maintain the timeline while deriving equations
write the cumulative effect of the transitions
distinguish one-time changes from repeated changes
distinguish simultaneous changes from sequential changes
operation_planning: choose operations only after naming the relation
state why addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division applies
connect multiplication to repeated equal groups when relevant
connect division to sharing or rate inversion when relevant
connect subtraction to difference or remaining quantity when relevant
connect addition to aggregation when relevant
plan the computation before carrying it out
write the operation sequence in symbolic form
preserve units through each planned operation
avoid collapsing distinct operations into one step
describe the arithmetic path before evaluating
state what each arithmetic step produces
intermediate_structure: organize the reasoning into a compact scaffold
expand the intermediate derivation before numeric work
show how the setup leads to the computation
keep intermediate meanings attached to intermediate values
write each intermediate value with its role
build the structure before selecting the shortest route
hold the structure together across several steps
state the role of each line in the derivation
keep the derivation centered on relationships
perspective_shift: reason from the target quantity backward to the givens
reason from the givens forward to the target quantity
compare the forward and backward dependency paths
view the situation as a conservation relation
view the situation as a rate relation
view the situation as a parts-and-whole relation
view the situation as a sequence of state updates
view the situation as a proportional mapping
translate between narrative perspective and equation perspective
compare direct computation with inverse computation
use the inverse relation to clarify the setup
use a different representation to expose hidden structure
